purpose: Craft examples for export-friendly short drama with culturally clear stakes, translation-ready dialogue, and relationship/status pressure.
source_priority_rules: Keep plot facts, roles, places, and rules source-grounded; this pack only sharpens readability and localization.
Prefer universally readable behavior and consequence over local idiom or meme phrasing.
Do not simplify by deleting source-grounded causality or role hierarchy.
genre_boundary: Make relationship or status pressure clear within one visible beat.
Use short, translation-ready lines with clean intent and minimal local shorthand.
Visual reversals should still track cause and effect, not just emotional shock.

scene_card_examples.visual: A status gap or relationship imbalance is visible before anyone explains it, and the next action makes the cost clear across languages.
scene_card_examples.voiceover: The audience should understand the power difference before they hear the argument.

asset_goal.purpose: status contrast, readable blocking, export-safe context

script_scene_examples.visual: One character chooses public politeness while privately escalating the conflict through a concrete action.
script_scene_examples.voiceover: The line stays simple; the action carries the threat.

dialogue.speaker: lead
dialogue.line: If you want the truth, stay and watch.

script_scene_examples.action_result: The scene ends on a clear hook that survives translation.

dialogue_style_examples: Prefer short intent-forward lines that survive direct translation.
Avoid culture-specific sayings when a clear action or threat can do the same job.

negative_examples.bad: The scene relies on a local internet joke to carry the payoff.
negative_examples.why_bad: That weakens overseas readability and translation quality.

forbidden_drift: Do not swap lead character identity, companion role, or speaker authority for easier localization.
Do not invent a new prop, object, menu item, ledger, or weapon to simplify exposition.
Do not replace source pressure with generic romance or generic suspense.
Do not relocate a scene, room, route, or blocking path unless the translated action still preserves source geography.
Do not overload lines with local idioms that block translation.
Do not erase relationship/status clarity in pursuit of brevity.

image: Lock readable status contrast, gesture clarity, and export-safe wardrobe/context cues.
video: Favor action, blocking, and reaction clarity that stays legible after dubbing or subtitle translation.
